item_name: Figma Free Tier — Asset Design + Composition + Handoff
definition_origin: Figma is a browser-based collaborative vector design tool, the de facto standard for UI/UX design since ~2020. The free 'Starter' plan exists to maximize developer/designer adoption. Critically for a solo dev, Figma's free tier is generous enough to ship a real product: 3 design files, unlimited drafts, full editing features, plugins, components, variants, Dev Mode (with read-only inspect), and community files. Figma replaces the 'hire-a-designer' line for asset composition, mascot variant management, icon system curation, and developer handoff.
core_purpose: Figma is the FINISHING and SYSTEMATIZING layer between AI-generated raw assets and shipped product. At the visceral level it lets you visually align icons on a 24px grid (the 'looks-right' polish). At the behavioral level component variants ensure every screen uses the same button/icon/illustration consistently. At the reflective level it documents the design system so future-you (or a contractor) can extend the brand without drift. For a solo dev with zero design skill: Figma is the place where you take Recraft's messy AI-SVG, snap it to a grid, recolor it with brand tokens, and export it as a production-ready icon component.
when_needed: Always — every consumer mobile, B2B/dev-tool, AI-wrapper, and marketing project benefits from Figma as the asset-curation hub. Especially needed when: (a) building a >5-screen mobile app (icon system mandatory); (b) needing 12+ mascot poses (component variants are essential); (c) handing off to AI-pair-programming via Figma-to-code plugins; (d) shipping a marketing landing page (Figma is the layout brain).
format_options: Figma exports: SVG (clean, optimized, Figma can pass through SVGO), PNG @1x/@2x/@3x/@4x, JPG, PDF, WebP. Imports: SVG, PNG, JPG, GIF, WebP. Plugins extend to Lottie (LottieFiles plugin), JSON, video frames. The export-on-save is configurable per layer (right-click > Copy as PNG / SVG). Figma's native vector editing produces cleaner SVGs than most AI tools because the path-creation respects intentionality. Critical: Figma uses 'frames' (artboards) and 'components' (reusable). For mobile assets, set frame at 1x and use 'Export 2x/3x' settings; for web SVG export, ensure 'Inside' stroke alignment for predictable rendering.
file_size_performance: Figma's SVG export is verbose by default — always pipe through SVGO (free at svgomg.net) before shipping. Use Figma's 'Export with size' multiplier intelligently: for mobile, export PNG @1x @2x @3x to support all DPIs; for web, single SVG is enough. Avoid auto-layout in exports (it adds wrapper groups). For Lottie animation, use the LottieFiles plugin to export — keeps file sizes small (<50KB typical for a UI animation).
free_tools: Figma Free 'Starter' plan: 3 Figma design files + unlimited drafts (drafts are private and unlimited — solo devs basically have unlimited files), 3 FigJam files, real-time collaboration up to small team, full plugin access, full Dev Mode (read-only inspect — cannot edit), full Community library access (millions of free templates and assets). Free essential plugins: ICONIFY (200K+ free icons across Phosphor, Heroicons, Material, Lucide), PHOSPHOR ICONS (clean, modern monoline), UNSPLASH (free royalty-free photos), REMOVE BG (background removal), VECTORIZE.AI (raster-to-vector inside Figma), LOTTIEFILES (animation library), FIGMA TO REACT NATIVE (community plugin for component code), HTML.TO.DESIGN (paste any URL to import design), AUTOFLOW (arrows for user-flow diagrams), CONTENT REEL (placeholder text/avatars), STARK (accessibility checker — contrast, color blindness simulation), ANIMA (free tier: HTML/React/Vue export). All free. Plus FREEPIK plugin and BLUSH for free illustrations.
paid_tools: Figma Professional ($15/mo or $12/mo annual per editor) — unlimited Figma files, unlimited version history, branching, libraries shared across files. WORTH IT when: you have >3 active projects or want shared design library across them. Figma Organization ($45/mo/editor) — overkill for solo dev. Anima Premium ($31/mo) for premium Figma-to-code with Tailwind/MUI components. Figma Make (AI-driven design-to-prototype, paid add-on) — recently launched, generates prototypes from prompts; consider only if dropping Vercel v0. Builder.io Visual Copilot ($24/mo+) for Figma-to-React-Native code. Most solo devs DO NOT need to upgrade — drafts are unlimited and the 3-file shared-team limit is rarely hit if you treat each project as a draft until launch.
gemini_pro_workflow: Gemini Pro complements Figma by: (1) generating raw assets (Imagen 4 illustrations, mascot raster) that you import into Figma for cleanup; (2) generating prompts for Figma-to-code AI plugins ('Gemini, write a Builder.io prompt to convert this Figma frame to React Native with NativeWind'); (3) using Gemini conversational image editing to iterate on a Figma export ('clean up this exported PNG, remove background, sharpen edges'); (4) using NotebookLM to ingest your design-system docs and generate consistent copy/component-API summaries; (5) Gemini can analyze a Figma screenshot and recommend improvements ('audit this screen for visual hierarchy issues'). Critical pattern: Figma is the SOURCE OF TRUTH for finalized assets; Gemini is the IDEATOR. Don't try to use Gemini to manage components — that's Figma's job.
workflow_steps: Step 1 (one-time, 30 min): Set up a Figma 'Drafts' workspace with one master design-system file containing color tokens, type scale, icon grid (24px), spacing scale (4/8/16/24/32). Step 2 (per asset, 5 min): Import AI-generated raw asset (paste SVG or drop PNG into Figma). Step 3 (5 min): Snap to 24px grid using Layout Grid; use Smart Guides for alignment. Step 4 (5 min): Recolor using design tokens (link fill to a Style instead of hex). Step 5 (10 min): Convert to Component (Cmd+Alt+K) with Variants for states/expressions/sizes (e.g. mascot 'happy/sad/excited/neutral' as variant property). Step 6 (5 min): Set Auto Layout for resilient resizing. Step 7 (5 min): Export — Right-click > Export as SVG (icons) or PNG @2x/@3x (raster assets). Step 8 (5 min): Run SVG through SVGO; drop into codebase. Step 9 (per session, 10 min): Use Dev Mode (top-right toggle) to inspect colors/measurements while implementing in code. Step 10 (per handoff): Use Anima or Builder.io plugin to generate React Native code stub from selected frame. Total time per asset after setup: ~30 min.
ai_prompt_templates: Builder.io / Anima handoff prompt: 'Convert this selected Figma frame to a React Native component using Expo, NativeWind for styling, props for [list]. Use the design tokens defined in tokens.ts for colors and spacing.' Figma Make / V0 prompt for mascot variants: 'Generate a 4x4 grid of facial expression variants for this character: happy, sad, excited, neutral, sleepy, surprised, angry, thinking, etc. Maintain identical proportions and color palette across all expressions.' Iconify search prompt: 'Find a [function] icon in Phosphor or Lucide style, monoline 1.5px, 24x24'. Stark accessibility audit: 'Check contrast for all text/background pairs in this frame against WCAG AA'.
claude_code_integration: Claude Code workflows with Figma: (1) Convert exported SVGs to typed React Native components — 'I have an SVG icon, generate a typed RN component using react-native-svg, with size and color props'; (2) Generate a design-token bridge from Figma Variables JSON export to TypeScript constants (tokens.ts); (3) Set up @svgr/webpack or react-native-svg-transformer config; (4) Write svgo.config.js with custom plugins; (5) Auto-generate Storybook stories for each component variant; (6) Implement Code Connect (Figma's CLI tool) so Dev Mode shows your real component code instead of auto-generated CSS — 'set up Code Connect for my Button component matching the Figma component'; (7) Use Figma's REST API (read-only on free tier) to fetch design tokens at build time. Pattern: Figma is the design source, Claude Code is the bridge that turns Figma artifacts into code.
emotional_design_integration: Figma itself is archetype-neutral but its USE expresses archetype. Caregiver: warm rounded components, generous padding (24-32px), soft shadows, hand-picked organic illustrations. Magician: tight grid discipline, geometric components, subtle animations via Smart Animate. Hero: bold contrast tokens, sharp corners (0-4px radius), confident type scale. Jester: high-saturation palette, playful component naming ('button-bonk' instead of 'button-primary'), variant explosion (8 emotion states for mascot). Sage: minimal token palette (3 grays + 1 accent), strict 8px grid, no decorative variants. Build a design-system master file that EXPRESSES the archetype before designing screens — the system shapes every downstream decision.
specific_examples: Linear's design system (publicly visible Figma community files) — Sage/Magician archetype, monoline 1.5px icons, restrictive token palette. Stripe's brand kit on Figma Community — Hero archetype with vibrant gradient cards. Notion's icon system (also in community) — Sage minimal monoline. Untitled UI Kit (free Figma community resource, 1M+ downloads) — comprehensive starting point. Tailwind UI components have a free Figma version. Figma Community search 'mobile app design system' returns 100s of free starter kits. Reference threads: r/FigmaDesign on Reddit, designcode.io tutorials by Meng To.
anti_ai_slop_traps: Figma-specific anti-slop traps: (1) Importing raw AI-SVG and shipping without grid-snap (icons feel 'floaty' on screen); (2) Skipping Variants and copy-pasting components (drift across screens); (3) Hardcoding hex values instead of using Styles/Variables (theme breaks); (4) Using auto-layout incorrectly (wrappers add SVG <g> noise on export); (5) Trusting AI plugins (Figma Make, Genius) blindly — their default aesthetic is the SAME generic blue-purple gradient cards; (6) Letting Iconify drop in 8 different icon families on the same screen (mix of Phosphor + Material + Lucide signals 'incoherent brand'). Counter-strategies: Pick ONE icon family and mass-import via Iconify with consistent stroke. Run Stark accessibility plugin on every screen. Keep design tokens to <8 colors total. Always toggle Variants > Variant property labeling so 'state' / 'size' / 'archetype' are explicit. The biggest anti-slop move is the manual 5-minute polish per asset: nudge to grid, align stroke caps, verify pixel-perfect at export size.
common_pitfalls: (1) Hitting the 3-file team limit at the worst time — keep all WIP in 'Drafts' (unlimited) and only promote to team file at launch. (2) Plugin overload — every plugin slows Figma; uninstall unused. (3) Component-vs-Frame confusion — assets must be Components to be reusable; Frames are just rectangles. (4) Variants explosion — 32 variant combinations slows the file; cap at ~12. (5) Forgetting to set 'Export' settings — assets appear in Figma but don't generate exportable PNG/SVG. (6) Not using Figma Variables (Modes) for light/dark themes — manual hex pairs are unmaintainable. (7) Treating Dev Mode as definitive — always sanity-check exported CSS against actual code. (8) Sharing files publicly without realizing Free tier exposes your design to anyone with link. (9) Spending 8 hours on a logo when a 1-hour version would do — iterate AI cheap, don't perfect early in Figma. (10) Forgetting that Free tier Dev Mode is read-only — developer can inspect but not annotate.
accessibility_notes: Use Stark plugin (free) to audit contrast ratios (WCAG AA = 4.5:1 for text). Use color-blindness simulator before shipping. Check focus states for all interactive components. Provide alt text in component metadata. Use Figma Variables (Modes) for light/dark theme parity. Test reduced-motion: any Figma prototype animation should have a static fallback in code.
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile in Figma: design at 1x (e.g. iPhone 14 = 390x844), export PNG@2x@3x for retina. Frame size matters because plugin code-generators infer responsive layout from frame dimensions. React Native handoff via Anima/Builder.io targets RN-specific primitives (View, Text, Image not div/span/img). Web in Figma: design at 1440px desktop frame, use Auto Layout extensively for responsive flexbox-style behavior. Web exports favor SVG inline + CSS classes; mobile exports favor PNG fallback + react-native-svg components.
sources: [Figma Pricing](https://www.figma.com/pricing/), [Figma Dev Mode Guide](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/15023124644247-Guide-to-Dev-Mode), [Figma Code Connect React Docs](https://developers.figma.com/docs/code-connect/react/), [Best Free Figma Plugins 2026](https://line25.com/articles/best-figma-plugins-for-web-designers/), [Top Figma Icon Plugins 2026 — Noun Project](https://blog.thenounproject.com/best-free-icon-plugin-figma-top-6-best-free-plugins-figma-icons-2026/), [Convert Figma to React Native — Builder.io](https://www.builder.io/blog/convert-figma-to-react-native), [Anima Figma to React Plugin](https://www.figma.com/community/plugin/857346721138427857/anima-figma-to-code-react-html-css-tailwind-mui-devmode-inspect-react-html-vue-css), [Figma to Code Design Tokens 2026](https://inhaq.com/blog/figma-to-code-design-engineer-workflow), [LogRocket Figma to React Native Guide](https://blog.logrocket.com/exporting-figma-designs-react-native/)
